title: Diversity-Adaptive Guidance for Diffusion Models: Conditioning Strength from Online Uncertainty and Repulsion
reasoning: Original Semantic Scholar retrieval returned zero titles mainly because of 429 failures.
Backup arXiv retrieval returned multiple related guidance and classifier-free guidance titles.

The idea combines adaptive classifier-free guidance, uncertainty from conditional-unconditional discrepancy, and batch-level repulsion. The title-level evidence does not prove exact duplication, but it clearly shows an existing related literature space.
Therefore the platform should not accept the original v2 provisional novel label as a strong novelty decision.
key_related_candidates: Rethinking Oversaturation in Classifier-Free Guidance via Low Frequency
Bridging the Gap: Addressing Discrepancies in Diffusion Model Training for Classifier-Free Guidance
Improving Sample Quality of Diffusion Models Using Self-Attention Guidance
Token Perturbation Guidance for Diffusion Models
Simple Guidance Mechanisms for Discrete Diffusion Models
recommended_next_step: Read abstracts/full papers for adaptive guidance, CFG oversaturation, guidance perturbation, and self-attention guidance before assigning novel=True.
